{{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! This is like a stone, too || Jinkyukyogoroku 201 |- | 192 || 398 || III || Naiden || 2:2 || 2 || 2 || People say that one candle offered by the poor is the same as a thousand || Naiden 2:2 |- | 160 || 399 || III || Naiden || 6:6 || 6 || 6 || In Konko Daijin’s way of faith, you are saved not through chanting || Naiden 6:6 |- | 382 || 400 || III || Naiden || 6:8 || 6 || 8 || While walking along the road or while sitting on the ridge between || Naiden 6:8 |} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikiquote|Konko Daijin}} {{Commons category|金光教教典}} *[https://www.livingwithkami.com/reading Konkokyo bibliography] {{Authority control}} [[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{wikidata link|Q134716277}} [[Category:19th-century non-fiction books]] [[Category:Books of quotations]] [[Category:Japanese anthologies]] [[Category:Japanese religious texts]] [[Category:Konkokyo]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[sn:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[sn:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Because Heaven and Earth are || Jinkyukyogoroku 104 |- | 124 || 378 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 105:1-2 || 105 || 1-2 || People have often wondered why a man who is honest, doesn’t do || Jinkyukyogoroku 105:1-2 |- | 337 || 379 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 116:3 || 116 || 3 || If you depend on Kami by practicing your faith, you need not worry || Jinkyukyogoroku 116:3 |- | 218 || 380 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 122 || 122 || || You should practice faith wholeheartedly while you are in good health || Jinkyukyogoroku 122 |- | 214 || 381 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 135:1-2 || 135 || 1-2 || Konko Daijin taught me, Those who practice faith should always || Jinkyukyogoroku 135:1-2 |- | 211 || 382 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 150 || 150 || || Trouble results when you try to do something by your own strength. || Jinkyukyogoroku 150 |- | 164 || 383 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 151 || 151 || || No matter how well-educated or how smart you are, merely having || Jinkyukyogoroku 151 |- | 77 || 384 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 152 || 152 || || If one is too intellectual, he sometimes fails to retain the blessings he || Jinkyukyogoroku 152 |- | 152 || 385 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 155 || 155 || || There are people who worship at the entrance of the hiromae because || Jinkyukyogoroku 155 |- | 120 || 386 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 156 || 156 || || Practicing patience for a thousand days is necessary to achieve any || Jinkyukyogoroku 156 |- | 35 || 387 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 163 || 163 || || People cannot receive divine blessings because of their lack of faith. || Jinkyukyogoroku 163 |- | 320 || 388 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 164 || 164 || || If you practice faith, you should not say bad things even idly. What || Jinkyukyogoroku 164 |- | 173 || 389 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 167:1-2 || 167 || 1-2 || You should not think that you must make a request for an important || Jinkyukyogoroku 167:1-2 |- | 385 || 390 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 168 || 168 || || One cold day, I met a pitiful old man on my way to the Hiromae. I || Jinkyukyogoroku 168 |- | 284 || 391 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 170:1-2 || 170 || 1-2 || People say that you will become a kami if you abandon your desires. || Jinkyukyogoroku 170:1-2 |- | 328 || 392 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 171:1-2 || 171 || 1-2 || Even iron wears out when used. Similarly, it is natural for the human || Jinkyukyogoroku 171: 1-2 |- | 268 || 393 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 184 || 184 || || Even a monkey sometimes falls from a tree, and great scholars make || Jinkyukyogoroku 184 |- | 217 || 394 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 185 || 185 || || “In this world, even a greedy and cruel person sometimes receives || Jinkyukyogoroku 185 |- | 264 || 395 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 191:3-5 || 191 || 3-5 || Those who have just started practicing faith, immediately get upset || Jinkyukyogoroku 191:3-5 |- | 221 || 396 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 198:1-2 || 198 || 1-2 || People exist because of Kami, and Kami exists because of people. || Jinkyukyogoroku 198:1-2 |- | 301 || 397 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 201 || 201 || || People speak of practicing faith together. This is like a stone, too || Jinkyukyogoroku 201 |- | 192 || 398 || III || Naiden || 2:2 || 2 || 2 || People say that one candle offered by the poor is the same as a thousand || Naiden 2:2 |- | 160 || 399 || III || Naiden || 6:6 || 6 || 6 || In Konko Daijin’s way of faith, you are saved not through chanting || Naiden 6:6 |- | 382 || 400 || III || Naiden || 6:8 || 6 || 8 || While walking along the road or while sitting on the ridge between || Naiden 6:8 |} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikiquote|Konko Daijin}} {{Commons category|金光教教典}} *[https://www.livingwithkami.com/reading Konkokyo bibliography] {{Authority control}} [[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{wikidata link|Q134716277}} [[Category:19th-century non-fiction books]] [[Category:Books of quotations]] [[Category:Japanese anthologies]] [[Category:Japanese religious texts]] [[Category:Konkokyo]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[sn:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). Some disciple names are repeated in both volumes. ;Volume 1 {{div col|style=font-size:90%;|colwidth=40em}} # {{nihongo|Aoi Saki|青井サキ|}} # {{nihongo|Ishihara Ginzō|石原銀造|}} # {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}} (1) # {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! This is like a stone, too || Jinkyukyogoroku 201 |- | 192 || 398 || III || Naiden || 2:2 || 2 || 2 || People say that one candle offered by the poor is the same as a thousand || Naiden 2:2 |- | 160 || 399 || III || Naiden || 6:6 || 6 || 6 || In Konko Daijin’s way of faith, you are saved not through chanting || Naiden 6:6 |- | 382 || 400 || III || Naiden || 6:8 || 6 || 8 || While walking along the road or while sitting on the ridge between || Naiden 6:8 |} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikiquote|Konko Daijin}} {{Commons category|金光教教典}} *[https://www.livingwithkami.com/reading Konkokyo bibliography] {{Authority control}} [[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{wikidata link|Q134716277}} [[Category:19th-century non-fiction books]] [[Category:Books of quotations]] [[Category:Japanese anthologies]] [[Category:Japanese religious texts]] [[Category:Konkokyo]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[sn:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). Some disciple names are repeated in both volumes. ;Volume 1 {{div col|style=font-size:90%;|colwidth=40em}} # {{nihongo|Aoi Saki|青井サキ|}} # {{nihongo|Ishihara Ginzō|石原銀造|}} # {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}} (1) # There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[sn:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). Some disciple names are repeated in both volumes. ;Volume 1 {{div col|style=font-size:90%;|colwidth=40em}} # {{nihongo|Aoi Saki|青井サキ|}} # {{nihongo|Ishihara Ginzō|石原銀造|}} # {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}} (1) # {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! This is like a stone, too || Jinkyukyogoroku 201 |- | 192 || 398 || III || Naiden || 2:2 || 2 || 2 || People say that one candle offered by the poor is the same as a thousand || Naiden 2:2 |- | 160 || 399 || III || Naiden || 6:6 || 6 || 6 || In Konko Daijin’s way of faith, you are saved not through chanting || Naiden 6:6 |- | 382 || 400 || III || Naiden || 6:8 || 6 || 8 || While walking along the road or while sitting on the ridge between || Naiden 6:8 |} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikiquote|Konko Daijin}} {{Commons category|金光教教典}} *[https://www.livingwithkami.com/reading Konkokyo bibliography] {{Authority control}} [[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{wikidata link|Q134716277}} [[Category:19th-century non-fiction books]] [[Category:Books of quotations]] [[Category:Japanese anthologies]] [[Category:Japanese religious texts]] [[Category:Konkokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[sn:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[Category:sn_crud commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Because Heaven and Earth are || Jinkyukyogoroku 104 |- | 124 || 378 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 105:1-2 || 105 || 1-2 || People have often wondered why a man who is honest, doesn’t do || Jinkyukyogoroku 105:1-2 |- | 337 || 379 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 116:3 || 116 || 3 || If you depend on Kami by practicing your faith, you need not worry || Jinkyukyogoroku 116:3 |- | 218 || 380 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 122 || 122 || || You should practice faith wholeheartedly while you are in good health || Jinkyukyogoroku 122 |- | 214 || 381 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 135:1-2 || 135 || 1-2 || Konko Daijin taught me, Those who practice faith should always || Jinkyukyogoroku 135:1-2 |- | 211 || 382 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 150 || 150 || || Trouble results when you try to do something by your own strength. || Jinkyukyogoroku 150 |- | 164 || 383 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 151 || 151 || || No matter how well-educated or how smart you are, merely having || Jinkyukyogoroku 151 |- | 77 || 384 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 152 || 152 || || If one is too intellectual, he sometimes fails to retain the blessings he || Jinkyukyogoroku 152 |- | 152 || 385 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 155 || 155 || || There are people who worship at the entrance of the hiromae because || Jinkyukyogoroku 155 |- | 120 || 386 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 156 || 156 || || Practicing patience for a thousand days is necessary to achieve any || Jinkyukyogoroku 156 |- | 35 || 387 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 163 || 163 || || People cannot receive divine blessings because of their lack of faith. || Jinkyukyogoroku 163 |- | 320 || 388 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 164 || 164 || || If you practice faith, you should not say bad things even idly. What || Jinkyukyogoroku 164 |- | 173 || 389 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 167:1-2 || 167 || 1-2 || You should not think that you must make a request for an important || Jinkyukyogoroku 167:1-2 |- | 385 || 390 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 168 || 168 || || One cold day, I met a pitiful old man on my way to the Hiromae. I || Jinkyukyogoroku 168 |- | 284 || 391 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 170:1-2 || 170 || 1-2 || People say that you will become a kami if you abandon your desires. || Jinkyukyogoroku 170:1-2 |- | 328 || 392 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 171:1-2 || 171 || 1-2 || Even iron wears out when used. Similarly, it is natural for the human || Jinkyukyogoroku 171: 1-2 |- | 268 || 393 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 184 || 184 || || Even a monkey sometimes falls from a tree, and great scholars make || Jinkyukyogoroku 184 |- | 217 || 394 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 185 || 185 || || “In this world, even a greedy and cruel person sometimes receives || Jinkyukyogoroku 185 |- | 264 || 395 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 191:3-5 || 191 || 3-5 || Those who have just started practicing faith, immediately get upset || Jinkyukyogoroku 191:3-5 |- | 221 || 396 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 198:1-2 || 198 || 1-2 || People exist because of Kami, and Kami exists because of people. || Jinkyukyogoroku 198:1-2 |- | 301 || 397 || III || Jinkyukyogoroku || 201 || 201 || || People speak of practicing faith together. This is like a stone, too || Jinkyukyogoroku 201 |- | 192 || 398 || III || Naiden || 2:2 || 2 || 2 || People say that one candle offered by the poor is the same as a thousand || Naiden 2:2 |- | 160 || 399 || III || Naiden || 6:6 || 6 || 6 || In Konko Daijin’s way of faith, you are saved not through chanting || Naiden 6:6 |- | 382 || 400 || III || Naiden || 6:8 || 6 || 8 || While walking along the road or while sitting on the ridge between || Naiden 6:8 |} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikiquote|Konko Daijin}} {{Commons category|金光教教典}} *[https://www.livingwithkami.com/reading Konkokyo bibliography] {{Authority control}} [[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{wikidata link|Q134716277}} [[Category:19th-century non-fiction books]] [[Category:Books of quotations]] [[Category:Japanese anthologies]] [[Category:Japanese religious texts]] [[Category:Konkokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). Some disciple names are repeated in both volumes. ;Volume 1 {{div col|style=font-size:90%;|colwidth=40em}} # {{nihongo|Aoi Saki|青井サキ|}} # {{nihongo|Ishihara Ginzō|石原銀造|}} # {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}} (1) # {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). Some disciple names are repeated in both volumes. ;Volume 1 {{div col|style=font-size:90%;|colwidth=40em}} # {{nihongo|Aoi Saki|青井サキ|}} # {{nihongo|Ishihara Ginzō|石原銀造|}} # {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}} (1) # There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). Some disciple names are repeated in both volumes. ;Volume 1 {{div col|style=font-size:90%;|colwidth=40em}} # {{nihongo|Aoi Saki|青井サキ|}} # {{nihongo|Ishihara Ginzō|石原銀造|}} # {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}} (1) # {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! This is like a stone, too || Jinkyukyogoroku 201 |- | 192 || 398 || III || Naiden || 2:2 || 2 || 2 || People say that one candle offered by the poor is the same as a thousand || Naiden 2:2 |- | 160 || 399 || III || Naiden || 6:6 || 6 || 6 || In Konko Daijin’s way of faith, you are saved not through chanting || Naiden 6:6 |- | 382 || 400 || III || Naiden || 6:8 || 6 || 8 || While walking along the road or while sitting on the ridge between || Naiden 6:8 |} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikiquote|Konko Daijin}} {{Commons category|金光教教典}} *[https://www.livingwithkami.com/reading Konkokyo bibliography] {{Authority control}} [[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{wikidata link|Q134716277}} [[Category:19th-century non-fiction books]] [[Category:Books of quotations]] [[Category:Japanese anthologies]] [[Category:Japanese religious texts]] [[Category:Konkokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" ! revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[co:commons:Category:金光教教典]][[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] [[en:Konkokyo Kyoten]] {{Short description|Main scripture of the Konkokyo religion}} {{Italic title}} {{Infobox religious text | name = {{Transliteration|ja|Konkōkyō Kyōten}} | subheader = {{lang|ja|金光教教典}} | image = | background = | alt = | caption = | religion = [[Konkokyo]] | author = Konkō Daijin, et al. | period = 19th century | language = Japanese | books = 5 | chapters = 240 | sutras = | verses = | native_wikisource = | orig_lang_code = ja | wikisource = | footnotes = }} The {{Nihongo||金光教教典|'''Konkōkyō Kyōten'''|{{lit}} "Konkokyo Book of Teachings"|lead=yes}} is the main religious scripture of [[Konkokyo]], a [[Shinto]]-derived Japanese religion founded in 1859 by {{nihongo|[[Konkō Daijin]]|金光大神}} (1814–1883). ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' is a compilation of various writings composed by Konkō Daijin and his disciples during the second half of the 19th century.{{cite web | title=Encyclopedia of Shinto | website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム | url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9816 | access-date=2025-06-05}} ==Contents== The ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' (金光教教典){{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=konkozaidan.jp | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-26}} is Konkokyo's primary scripture. It consists of three parts, of which the third part, ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"), is the largest one.{{cite book |title=Shine from within: An introduction to the Konko Faith |author=Konkokyo |date=2009 |location=Konko, Okayama |publisher=Konkokyo |oclc=61459011}} #''Kyōten Konkō Daijin Oboegaki'' (金光大神御覚書, "Memoirs of Konko Daijin"): 25 chapters, which Konko Daijin began writing starting from November 23, 1874. #''Kyōten Oshirase-goto Oboe-chō'' (お知らせ事覚帳, "Record of Revelations"): 27 chapters, one for each year from 1857 to 1883. #''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集, "Teachings of Konko Daijin"): This part, by far the largest one, consists of 3 volumes. It consists of Konkō Daijin's orally transmitted teachings as written down by his various disciples. ##Volume 1: 27 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple. There are 25 disciples total, since there are 3 sections for one of the disciples, {{nihongo|Ichimura Mitsugorō|市村光五郎|}}. ##Volume 2: 153 sections ordered according to the name of the disciple ##Volume 3: 8 sections ###{{nihongo|''Omichi Annai''|御道案内}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkai''|慎誡}} ###{{nihongo|''Kyōso Gorikai''|教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Shinkun''|神訓}} ###{{nihongo|''Konkō Kyōso Gorikai''|金光教祖御理解}} ###{{nihongo|''Gorikai Shūi''|御理解拾遺}} ###{{nihongo|''Jinkyūkyō Goroku''|尋求教語録}} ###{{nihongo|''Naiden''|内伝}} In addition to the main book, there are also the following supplemental volumes:{{cite web | title=書籍案内 | website=金光教公式サイト | date=2025-03-10 | url=https://www.konkokyo.jp/faith/teaching/books/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}} *Index (用語索引) *Announcements (お知らせ事覚書帳注釈) (annotated historical notices and memoranda) *Biographical dictionary (人物誌) *Glossary (用語事典) *Supplement (追補) (122 verses of teachings that 12 followers had received from Konko Daijin) ==Editions== There is a 1983 Japanese edition of the main volume of the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'', as well as a more recent revised Japanese edition published in 2009. The English edition is published as 5 separate volumes:{{cite web | title=金光教教典 | website=金光教徒社 | date=2009-04-28 | url=http://www.konkozaidan.jp/kyotosya/books/data_books/t1240898693/index_html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} *''Kyoten Konko Daijin Oboegaki: Memoirs of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御覚書) *''Kyoten Oshirase-goto Oboe-cho: Record of Revelations'' (お知らせ事覚帳) *''Kyoten Gorikai: Teachings of Konko Daijin'' (金光大神御理解集) (3 volumes) ==List of disciples== Below is a list of disciples in volumes 1 and 2 of the ''Kyōten Gorikai'' (金光大神御理解集). {{nihongo|Watanabe Tome|渡辺登免|}} # {{nihongo|(unknown)|伝承者不明|}} {{div col end}} ==''Tenchi wa kataru''== 400 selected passages from the ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' are organized topically in {{nihongo|''Tenchi wa kataru''|天地は語る|}}, a book published by the Konkokyo headquarters.{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=あなたの心を元気にするページ | url=https://www.ko-ougimachi.com/tenti/tenti.htm | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}{{cite web | title=天地は語る | website=金光教下松教会 | url=https://kudamatsu10.web.fc2.com/tenchi.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}} The English translation is titled ''Voice of the Universe: Selected teachings of Konkokyo'', while the Portuguese translation, published in 2008, is titled ''A Voz do Universo: Ensinamentos selecionados de Konkokyo''. There are also Spanish and Korean translations of ''Tenchi wa kataru''.{{cite web | title=Publications | website=Konkokyo | url=https://konkokyo.jp/eng/our_faith/publications.html | access-date=2025-06-05}} The table below gives the corresponding ''Konkōkyō Kyōten'' verse numbers for each of the 400 quotations in ''Voice of the Universe'' (天地は語る). {| class="wikitable sortable" !
